REPLY TO NON OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF GOSK, JENNIFER
MINI MINUTES FOR AUG-02-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: CROSS DEFENDANT CENTURY CAMPUS HOUSING MANAGEMENT. L.P.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. CLV DID NOT MEET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF EVIDENCE TO DEMONSTRATE THAT ABSENCE OF A MATERIAL ISSUE OF FACT AND THAT IT IS THUS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. ITEMS 6 AND 7 AT PAGE 5 OF THE CROSS-DEFENDANTS MEMORANDUM, AND UMF 13, TAKEN TOGETHER ARE SUFFICIENT TO OBVIATE THE MOVING PARTIES' ARGUMENT REGARDING THE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT. AAU CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING CLV'S NEGLIGENCE. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT REGARDING WHETHER CLV WAS AWARE OF THE DANGEROUS CONDITION IN THE BATHROOM AND FAILED TO ATTEMPT TO REMEDY IT IN A TIMELY FASHION. PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED THAT THE LEAKY SINK WAS A PERENNIAL PROBLEM AND THE RESIDENT SUPERVISORS (DANNY HALL AND JEANNETTE ESTEVES) WERE AWARE OF THE PROBLEM PRIOR TO THE INCIDENT. (P'S DEPO, 107:1-24, 117:18-120:7, 120:18-121:13, 141:13-18, 141:19-142:14, 150:23-151:4). CLV'S PMK (DR. DORIS COLLINS) TESTIFIED THAT SHE DID NOT KNOW WHEN INSPECTIONS OF THE BATHROOM WERE MADE AND NOBODY REPORTED A LEAK A WEEK PRIOR TO THE ACCIDENT. (COLLINS DEPO, 61:21 - 67:10; 81:3-23). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD A. KRAMER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN CSR6185.

DBA ACADEMY OF ART'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. WHEN PLAINTIFF FILED HER FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, PLAINTIFF EXCEEDED THE CLEAR TERMS OF THE ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT TO NAME DOE DEFENDANT, FILED FEBRUARY 7, 2013. IF PLAINTIFF SOUGHT TO DO MORE THAN NAME A DOE DEFENDANT, SHE WAS REQUIRED TO GIVE NOTICE OF SUCH AMENDMENTS, ATTACH A PROPOSED PLEADING, AND COMPLY WITH RULE OF COURT 3.1324. THAT DID NOT HAPPEN IN THIS INSTANCE. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR PLAINTIFF SEEKING LEAVE TO FILE AN AMENDED COMPLAINT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. Cross Defendant Century Campus Housing Management. L.P.'S Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-13-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MATTHEW BARNETTE, ESQ., 925.930.6600, FOR CROSS DEFENDANT CENTURY CAMPUS HOUSING MANAGEMEMT, L.P.
STACEY LEASK, ESQ., 415.391.1155, FOR DEFENDANT/CROSS COMPLAINANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE INC.., DBA ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: Cross Defendant Century Campus Housing Management. L.P.'S Motion For Summary Judgment is denied. CLV did not meet its burden of production of evidence to demonstrate that absence of a material issue of fact and that it is thus entitled to judgment as a matter of law. Items 6 and 7 at page 5 of the Cross-Defendants memorandum, and UMF 13, taken together are sufficient to obviate the moving parties' argument regarding the issue of material fact. AAU creates a triable issue of material fact regarding CLV's negligence. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether CLV was aware of the dangerous condition in the bathroom and failed to attempt to remedy it in a timely fashion. Plaintiff testified that the leaky sink was a perennial problem and the resident supervisors (Danny Hall and Jeannette Esteves) were aware of the problem prior to the incident. (P's depo, 107:1-24, 117:18-120:7, 120:18-121:13, 141:13-18, 141:19-142:14, 150:23-151:4). CLV's PMK (Dr. Doris Collins) testified that she did not know when inspections of the bathroom were made and nobody reported a leak a week prior to the accident. (Collins depo, 61:21 - 67:10; 81:3-23). Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard A. Kramer, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn CSR6185.

ATTY. SHARON COLLIER OF ARCHER NORRIS, TEL. NO. (925) 930-6600 EXT. 295 APPEARING FOR THE CROSS-DEFENDANTS CENTURY CAMPUS HOUSING MANAGEMENT BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE, INC. DBA ACADEMY OF ART's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT - The motion to strike is granted, with leave to amend. When Plaintiff filed her First Amended Complaint, Plaintiff exceeded the clear terms of the Order Granting Plaintiff's Motion to Amend Complaint to Name Doe Defendant, filed February 7, 2013. If Plaintiff sought to do more than name a Doe defendant, she was required to give notice of such amendments, attach a proposed pleading, and comply with Rule of Court 3.1324. That did not happen in this instance. This order is without prejudice for Plaintiff seeking leave to file an amended complaint. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
